methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Dronamics, a Series A stage operator in Drones & Autonomous, has raised $43.2M to date. The Drones & Autonomous sector comprises eight private peers including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker, with no public comparables. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting filtering criteria—only one adjacent-stage comparable exists within the sector, below the three-point threshold required for reliable median derivation.

The stage-progression method was therefore applied as the primary valuation lever. Using total capital raised ($43M) as a base value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression to the next funding stage, this approach yields an implied valuation of $86M. This method directly reflects the company's demonstrated ability to secure institutional capital and the expected value creation between Series A and Series B financings.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $73M to $99M, centered on $86M. This range is bounded by conservative and optimistic interpretations of stage-typical progression multiples but relies on a single applicable method due to peer set constraints. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable exits or later-stage financings in the autonomous aircraft vertical, sector-level funding market conditions, and Dronamics' specific operational traction relative to the peer cohort. Additional diligence on revenue, customer commitments, and technical milestones would sharpen conviction within this range.
